description:
You are the system administrator of a small company.  
A recent audit discovered that one of your Web-server configuration files is leaking version information, because the directive  <server_tokens on;> is enabled.  
Your task is to:

1. Edit the file  
   /home/user/nginx.conf  
   so that the directive

        server_tokens on;

   is replaced by

        server_tokens off;

   • Keep the rest of the file exactly as it is.  
   • There must be exactly one space between “server_tokens” and “off;”.

2. Create (or overwrite, if it already exists) the log file  
   /home/user/security_patch.log  
   containing exactly one line (plus the terminating newline) in this format:

        PATCH_APPLIED: server_tokens off

   • No leading or trailing spaces.  
   • No additional lines or characters.

Verification rules (strict):
• The file /home/user/nginx.conf must contain the line “server_tokens off;” and must no longer contain the substring “server_tokens on;”.  
• The file /home/user/security_patch.log must exist and its entire content must be exactly the 35-byte sequence:  
  “PATCH_APPLIED: server_tokens off\n” (the final newline character is mandatory).

Once these two conditions are met, the task is complete.
truth:
Initial state before the agent begins:

1. File /home/user/nginx.conf exists with the following contents (12 bytes of UTF-8 text followed by a newline):
server_tokens on;

2. File /home/user/security_patch.log does NOT exist.

Required final state after successful completion:

1. File /home/user/nginx.conf must contain (15 bytes of UTF-8 text followed by a newline):
server_tokens off;

2. File /home/user/security_patch.log must contain (35 bytes including the trailing newline):
PATCH_APPLIED: server_tokens off

(There must be exactly one trailing newline and no other characters.)
name: task_000000_890ec533
